af_score_components.notes: 0nMCP universal AI orchestrator with 564 tools across 26 services. Has MCP server with comprehensive tool coverage. Documentation exists but scattered across multiple service docs. Auth varies by underlying service (API keys, OAuth). Rate limits inherited from wrapped services. MCP quality 85 — mature integration layer.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS for all service calls. Auth complexity high — manages credentials for 26 different services. Service-dependent scope granularity. Secret storage depends on deployment (env vars recommended). Orchestration layer doesn't provide additional auth boundary. Risk: credential accumulation across multiple service integrations.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Reliability depends entirely on wrapped services. Single point of orchestration failure. Service heterogeneity means failure modes vary widely. No SLA (community project). Error recovery depends on individual service retry logic. Suitable for development/testing across multiple APIs but not production-critical unified interface.

af_score_components.notes: 0xHumans Protocol MCP for DeFi operations on Base blockchain. Has MCP server (quality 70). Documentation minimal — developer focused on blockchain context. Blockchain operations are inherently async. Rate limits depend on RPC provider. Very specialized use case (financing/lending protocols). auth_complexity=75 for wallet/signing requirements.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS for all API calls. Blockchain-based auth with private key management (very strong cryptography). Smart contract scope controls via role-based access on-chain. Cryptocurrency-grade security practices. Secret handling critical — private keys must be protected. Audit trail on-chain provides transparency.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Relies on Base blockchain uptime (solid but not guaranteed). Smart contract state changes are permanent (no undo). Transaction finality requires block confirmations. RPC provider reliability is critical. Early-stage protocol integration adds instability risk. Suitable for DeFi agents but requires patience for on-chain confirmation times.

af_score_components.notes: 123elec MCP for French electronics retailer API. Has MCP server (quality 60). Documentation in French, limited English support. Wraps existing retailer API. Rate limits undocumented. Regional specificity (France only) limits generalizability. auth_complexity=70 for retailer-specific authentication.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. API key auth standard. No fine-grained scoping. Retailer-grade security (adequate for commerce). Language barrier reduces security documentation accessibility. Payload data contains customer/order info requiring protection. No PCI compliance info available.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Depends on 123elec retailer API stability. No SLA information. Regional focus creates fragility for international deployments. Language-specific interface limits troubleshooting. Inventory operations are async. No idempotency guarantees. Community-maintained wrapper adds latency to security updates.

af_score_components.notes: particlefuture/MCPDiscovery — MCP server discovery service. Has MCP (quality 55). Minimal documentation. Metadata-driven approach. No inherent auth (discovery layer). Rate limits unclear. Very early stage. Essentially a registry tool.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS recommended but not enforced. No auth by design (public registry). Metadata about other MCPs is public. Trust model depends on upstream MCP security. Discovery of unknown MCPs carries unknown risk. Suitable for local/private use but not for public-facing agent deployments.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Very early-stage project. Registry reliability depends on metadata freshness. No SLA. Metadata stale-ness can cause discovery of broken MCPs. Community project with uncertain long-term maintenance. Suitable for experimentation only.

af_score_components.notes: 1Panel — open-source VPS control panel with MCP. Has MCP server (quality 70). Self-hosted dashboard provides REST API. OpenAPI spec available. Bearer token auth. One-click OpenClaw deployment mentioned. Targeted at infrastructure ops.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced for dashboard. Bearer token auth with reasonable strength. Self-hosted security posture (operator responsible). Manages cloud credentials and infrastructure access (high privilege). Webhook support available. No formal compliance certifications but open-source allows audit.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Self-hosted so reliability is operator-managed. Open-source project with active community. Infrastructure operations are async (provisioning takes time). Retry logic needed for infrastructure changes. OpenAPI spec provides stability. Suitable for operators building AI infrastructure automation.

af_score_components.notes: 1Password MCP for service accounts. Has MCP server (quality 80). Official 1Password offering. Excellent documentation. Service account auth model. Rate limits documented. Focused on vault and item access operations.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. Service accounts with granular vault-level permissions. 1Password infrastructure (SOC2 certified, enterprise security). Zero-knowledge encryption for vault contents. Official project with security focus. Logging practices prevent secret exposure. Industry-leading for secret management.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: 1Password infrastructure — enterprise SLA and status page. Official support. Service account model provides stability. SDK maintained across languages. Retry guidance documented. Full idempotency for reads. Excellent reliability for credential/secret use cases.

af_score_components.notes: 1stDibs marketplace MCP for browsing/searching antiques. Has MCP server (quality 65). Wraps public API. Documentation adequate for e-commerce context. API key auth. Rate limits moderate. Marketplace specific (1stDibs only).

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. API key authentication. Limited scoping (read-only for public API). Marketplace-grade security. Customer data exposure risk (browsing history, saved items). No audit trail control. Community wrapper, not official 1stDibs project.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: 1stDibs marketplace stability (reasonable but consumer-focused). No SLA for API. Inventory data real-time but can change rapidly. Search reliability depends on catalog index freshness. Unofficial wrapper adds latency risk. Suitable for agents doing marketplace browsing, not critical operations.

af_score_components.notes: 1trip PULSE — travel planning MCP with 21 integrated tools. Has MCP server (quality 75). Multi-tool integration (flights, hotels, visa, safety, weather). Documentation adequate. Auth varies by wrapped service. Rate limits inherited. Purpose-built for travel agents.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS for all service calls. Multiple service integrations (credential management complexity). Travel data contains sensitive personal information (passport, visa, itinerary). No centralized scope control (varies by service). Aggregation layer doesn't strengthen individual service auth. Risk: multiple credential sets across travel services.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Reliability depends on 21 integrated travel services (high failure complexity). No SLA. Individual service failures affect planning quality. International travel operations (timezone-dependent). Retry logic essential. Suitable for personal travel planning but not mission-critical operations.

af_score_components.notes: 1xn vMCP — Virtual Model Context Protocol. Has MCP (quality 50). Minimal documentation. Concept-stage project. No clear auth model (appears experimental). Rate limits unknown. Very early exploratory work. Uncertain purpose/scope.

security_score_components.security_notes: Not security-hardened. Experimental project. No formal security review. Auth model unclear/unimplemented. Not suitable for sensitive operations. Early-stage research project. Requires significant security work before production use.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Experimental/research stage. No reliability guarantees. No SLA. Suitable for exploration and prototyping only. Not ready for production use. Uncertain future maintenance.

af_score_components.notes: Bilibili creator tracking MCP. Has MCP server (quality 60). Bilibili API wrapper for video/dynamic tracking. Documentation limited (Chinese-focused). Rate limits inherited from Bilibili. Regional specificity (China-focused). Platform ToS compliance unclear for automated access.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS for Bilibili API calls. Cookie-based or token auth. Limited scoping. Regional platform (compliance varies). Scraping-adjacent operations may violate Bilibili ToS. Public data but aggregation creates tracking surface. Community wrapper (not official).

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Depends on Bilibili API stability. Platform API can change without notice. No SLA. Regional platform reliability varies. Community-maintained wrapper adds latency. Suitable for personal creator tracking, not for critical applications. ToS compliance risk.
